title: tanarius cornutus
intro: Tanarius cornutus, also known as Tanacetum cornutum, is a perennial plant in the sunflower family native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The plant contains sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Tanarius cornutus include relief from headaches, fever, and digestive issues, as well as its potential use as an antiseptic to prevent wound infections. Herbal preparations of Tanarius cornutus can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
